_comment: Corrected routing-model benchmark case study. This deck intentionally reports negative/blocked results instead of estimated savings.

scenes.title: Routing Model Benchmarks
scenes.subtitle: Real fixture results, not estimated savings

scenes.lede: The previous report was not acceptable evidence.
scenes.body: It mixed mined corpus counts, existing eval-pilot artifacts, and model-price estimates. The corrected case study only treats live fixture runs as benchmark evidence.

scenes.lede: The real Codex run chose the right intent name but missed slot fidelity.
scenes.body: Five failed inputs submitted refine_purchase with slots that did not satisfy the authored fixture contract. Room routing benchmarks must score slots, not just intent labels.

scenes.lede: The fixture path now supports real model-backed routing runs.
scenes.body: kitsoki test intents can run --harness claude, --agent codex, and --profile from .kitsoki.yaml/.kitsoki.local.yaml. Reports include harness metadata, elapsed time, actual intent, actual slots, and errors.

scenes.lede: Do not promote a cheaper router yet.
scenes.body: The mechanism is still valid: deterministic tiers first, cheapest passing room model second, stronger fallback on uncertainty. This benchmark says the candidate evidence is not ready.
